examples.title: Insertion at Beginning - Singly Linked List
examples.description: Algorithm to insert a new node at the beginning of a singly linked list
examples.code:
PROCEDURE insertAtBeginning(head, data)
  newNode ← allocate memory
  newNode.data ← data
  newNode.next ← head
  head ← newNode
  RETURN head
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Creates a new node, makes it point to current head, then updates head to new node. This makes the new node the first element in the list.

examples.title: Complete Linked List Implementation in C
examples.description: C program demonstrating linked list creation and basic operations
examples.explanation: Complete C implementation showing node structure, insertion at beginning and end, deletion from beginning, and display operations using malloc for dynamic memory allocation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for insert at beginning and delete; O(n) for insert at end and display

examples.title: Linked List Implementation in Python
examples.description: Python class implementation of singly linked list
examples.explanation: Python implementation using classes. Node class holds data and next reference. LinkedList class manages head and operations. Uses None instead of NULL.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for insert at beginning and delete; O(n) for insert at end, display, and reverse

examples.title: Reversing a Singly Linked List
examples.description: Iterative algorithm to reverse linked list using three pointers
examples.code:
PROCEDURE reverseList(head)
  prev ← NULL
  current ← head
  
  WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
    nextNode ← current.next
    current.next ← prev
    prev ← current
    current ← nextNode
  END WHILE
  
  RETURN prev  // new head
END PROCEDURE

// RECURSIVE VERSION
PROCEDURE reverseRecursive(node)
  IF node = NULL OR node.next = NULL THEN
    RETURN node
  END IF
  
  newHead ← reverseRecursive(node.next)
  node.next.next ← node
  node.next ← NULL
  RETURN newHead
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Iterative: Uses three pointers (prev, current, next) to reverse links one by one. Recursive: Base case returns node, then reverses links in unwinding phase. Both result in reversed list.
